projects.name: MENA to the Valley 2026
projects.company_brief: Sprintly Partners' flagship founder bridge program is now managed as a live operating tracker across cohort selection, payment follow-up, founder onboarding, mentor programming, logistics, and partner coordination. The record now reflects the full accessible source set around the cohort: applicant pipeline, pre-selected founders who received deposit requests, replacement contact handling for Roboost, and downstream onboarding dependencies including visas, travel, and session planning.

projects.notes: This project was upgraded again to better reflect the full accessible source set tied to the MENA to the Valley cohort. I could not authenticate into the shared OneDrive workbook from this environment, so I could not directly inspect the live spreadsheet tabs. Instead, I consolidated every locally available cohort source that appears to mirror pieces of that sheet: the March 11 application summary, the 12 individualized pre-March deposit emails, the cohort BCC draft, and the 16 founder selection email draft set. That let me add the missing replacement contact for Roboost, formalize the pre-selected founder cohort, expose onboarding dependencies after deposit receipt, and turn the project into a fuller operating tracker rather than a static applicant list. The remaining payment reconciliation, acceptance, visa, and travel fields still require the authenticated live workbook import to be final.

projects.name: ARPU Plus / CloudCom
projects.company_brief: ARPU Plus is a telecoms value-added services company under A15. Sprintly is supporting US business development and market expansion for portfolio products, with current focus on CloudCom / CloudCom Verify. CloudCom appears to be a broader omnichannel CPaaS and mobile identity platform, with Verify as the authentication wedge product.
projects.key_metrics: US positioning + target account mapping underway

timeline.milestone: Agreement signed
Initial CloudCom / Verify research brief completed

Map Yousra warm intros to best-fit fintech, telecom, and mobile-first enterprise prospects
Extract hard metrics and case studies from CloudCom deck / slides
Decide whether Verify is sold as standalone wedge or broader CloudCom platform story
Clarify the first US vertical and exact use case to lead with
Prepare a one-page external positioning summary for outreach

projects.notes:
Consolidated account view: CloudCom appears to be more than a single auth tool. Best current read is an omnichannel CPaaS + mobile identity platform, with Verify as the likely wedge product for US conversations. What they seem to sell in plain English is lower OTP cost, less login and onboarding friction, stronger authentication, and better coverage in hard-to-reach markets. The category is real and maps to Telesign, Prove, TMT ID, Vonage, Twilio, Infobip, and Open Gateway-style number verification.

What Shady Aziz confirmed by email: we can mention Google and local banks externally; Meta, Xiaomi, FedEx, and Amazon can only be described as indirect partners. For proof, he pointed us back to the product slides for impact / pain-point evidence. On competitors, he said they sometimes collaborate with aggregators by providing coverage, and sometimes compete directly for the same client. Their strongest differentiator is coverage, especially in difficult destinations across MENA, Asia, and Africa. On the commercial pain point, his clearest answer was that OTP can cost up to $0.30 in some markets, so cost + coverage is a sharper story than generic security language.

Current best positioning: CloudCom helps enterprises reduce OTP cost and improve authentication coverage, especially in high-cost and harder-to-reach markets. That is more concrete than describing them vaguely as an identity platform. The strongest story is not just fraud reduction. It is coverage in hard markets, lower OTP spend, and smoother mobile verification.

Intro / connection history is now tracked in three buckets. Already in conversation: Synapse Analytics, Money Fellows, e&, Contact, and Silicon Valley Bank. Reached out: Mathilda Akintayo (Uber), Hamed Rabah (Microsoft for Startups), Ahmed Nouh (Amazon), Waleed Abdelsalam (Huawei Northern Africa Region), Joyce Zhang (Visa), Mar Perez (Commercial Bank of California), and Enji Hamed (Amazon). Next wave: Tosh Ernest (Silicon Valley Bank), Sara Rona (Silicon Valley Bank), Alex Kearns (Citizens Private Bank), Yousra Abdelghany (Amazon Payment Services), Dalia Seif (TikTok), and Rania Hadi (Huawei). This needs to stay visible in the project so we know what already moved versus what is still pending.

What still needs answering: which US vertical to target first, which exact use case to lead with, whether Verify is sold standalone or as part of the broader CloudCom platform, stronger hard metrics from case studies, clearer pricing logic, implementation effort, and what we should avoid overpromising.

Commercial context: Retainer model with revenue share on partnerships introduced. Sprintly role is US business development and market expansion support for ARPU Plus portfolio products, starting with CloudCom.

projects.company_brief: Money Fellows is exploring US market entry with Sprintly supporting market strategy, segment prioritization, regulatory diligence, and go-to-market planning for a focused launch path.

Build the financial model covering launch budget, ROI timeline, customer economics, and pricing strategy

Develop a localized marketing plan using native language, community terminology, and community-event-based acquisition tactics
Draft the final strategy document for next week with segment recommendations, financial model, and marketing plan
projects.notes:
Operating truth from the Apr 20, 2026 recap: the viable US target segments are immigrant Rosca communities, credit invisible Americans, gig workers, and Muslim Americans. The strongest initial focus should be Muslim Americans and immigrant Rosca communities, with a clear Sharia-compliant emphasis and messaging that feels culturally native rather than translated. Go-to-market should be localized through the actual terms these communities use and through community events and trusted channels, not generic mass-market performance marketing. The strategy now requires two parallel workstreams before external execution: a financial model covering budget, ROI timeline, and pricing logic, and a licensing/regulatory workstream to determine how Money Fellows can legally and practically enter the US market. The output due next week is a clean strategy document that recommends segment priority, includes the financial model, and lays out the marketing plan.

Team structure update: the client-side working team is captured as the Money Fellows team, while Berkeley is recorded as Sprintly's supporting team on this workstream. Based on the verified meeting/calendar context currently available in the workspace, Yousra is the clearest confirmed active coordination owner because she hosted the Apr 20 meeting and is the only explicitly verified participant in the source recap. The named Money Fellows-side day-to-day point of contact still needs explicit confirmation, so the project record keeps that wording careful rather than overstating certainty.
